The Alumni Association plays a pivotal role in fostering a strong bond between the institution and its graduates. With a wide range of activities, the association ensures that the alumni remain connected to their alma mater and contribute to its growth and development. One of the most anticipated events organized by the association is the Annual Alumni Reunion, which brings together graduates from different batches, creating a platform for networking and reminiscing about their college days. Jai Hind College is beaming with pride as we celebrate 75 years of nurturing minds and shaping futures.To honor this special occasion, we hosted our largest annual alumni meet, a heartwarming gathering of Jai Hindites from all walks of life. It was a joyous celebration of our shared history, filled with stories of success and camaraderie. This event rekindled old friendships and inspired new dreams,
history, filled with stories of success and camaraderie. This event rekindled old friendships and inspired new dreams, reflecting the enduring spirit of our beloved college.

Newsletter
The very popular Newsletter of the Alumni Association titled “Circle of Friendship ” is released digitally to the entire global database of Alumni as a quarterly magazine. The Newsletter has completed five successful years of its journey & the readership is scaling new heights.

Faculty Advisor – Dr Rakhi Sharma
Since its inception, Jai Hind College (Autonomous) has fervently advocated for fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem within our society. In line with this vision, the “Entrepreneurship Cell and Skill Hub,” was introduced and digitally inaugurated by our Hon’ble Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi. This initiative is dedicated to empowering job-seekers to become tomorrow’s job-makers through continuous reskilling and hands-on learning experiences. With a keen focus on revitalizing the startup economy, our goal is to bolster the nation’s economic growth. Our vision is to provide aspiring entrepreneurs with the essential platforms and resources needed to transform their innovative ideas into leading businesses of the future, paving the way for groundbreaking advancements and sustainable success.

Entrepreneurship Cell and Skill Hub 2024-2025
Entrepreneurship Cell and Skill Hub – 2022-23
Glimpses From The Global E-Summit 2023
Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi Sharma

Entrepreneurship Cell and Skill Hub – 2022-23

Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi Sharma
One of the only committees on campus that blends business with culture, the best of both worlds. The Jai Hind Business Conclave, a BBA (Bachelors of Business Administration) initiative, is a thrilling two-day corporate event packed with management games, speaker sessions, panel discussions and engaging podcasts.This committee aims at bridging the gap between classroom learning and the real world and provides a unique platform for industry leaders business heads and the policymakers of our country to drive forward innovative ideas and foster a shared vision for a better, brighter and rejuvenated idea. From pre-events at the National Stock Exchange to interaction with the head honchos at Dr. Vaidya, Kelloggs, Deloitte, SBI Mutual Funds and more, JBC has it all.

JBC Report 2024-2025
Faculty Advisor – Dr Rakhi Sharma
Founded in the year 2020, the Jai Hind College Incubator & Accelerator Centre is a dedicated space for student startups to thrive. They offer:– Personalized mentoring by industry experts– Modern co-working spaces– Specialized programs to develop entrepreneurial skills– Opportunities to pitch to investorsTheir flagship event, “The Startup Exposition,” offers a platform for startups to showcase their ideas and connect with potential investors. “Intellectus” sessions educate startups on intellectual property rights, while their collaboration with the “Wadhwani Foundation” offers comprehensive support through online and offline programs.

Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi SharmaEnactus Jai Hind College, part of the global Enactus network, uses entrepreneurship for social and environmental good. Since joining in 2015, they have launched impactful projects like Tech Shiksha for 2023-2024, which provides education to underprivileged individuals offline. This initiative offers a chance to make a positive societal impact and helps develop a well-rounded skill set, fostering creativity and emotional intelligence.
Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi Sharma

Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi Sharma

Faculty Advisor: Dr Rakhi SharmaStudent Secretary-Moin Saiyad
Projects Under Enactus -Jai Hind CollegePROJECT GREEN TEE
Project Green Tee, is a quest born from the fraught uncertainties bestowed by the pandemic, to impart opportunities for alternative employment, financial independence, and personal empowerment, to women trudging in fragile hope through the landscape of some parts of Mumbai’s.

This initiative served two purpose – humanity and sustainability . It featured the participation of this marginalized community to recycle single-use plastic bottles into hand-sewn, stylish recycled t-shirt garments fit for everyday use.
HOW PROJECT GREEN TEE WORKS

We tackled a mass scale outreach effort to obtain plastic bottles from personal sources to external ones the likes of popular hotel chains. These bottles were then further segregated according to color, with their caps and labels removed, and shredded into small colorful chips. Following a warm rinse and trip to the ovens, the flakes were melted into tiny pellets, undergoing stringent filtration and contamination removal. The filaments are combined and winded into stronger threads to create weighted spools of yarn. The yarn is subsequently stretched, carded, and roved before it is spun into fabric and is sent to our beneficiaries workplace and then the fabric is sewn into our Green – Tees by our beneficiaries . And this is how 7 PET bottles can be transformed into 1 – uber comfortable, stylish t – shirt. Our team of over 120 volunteers were trained to promote sales within their

1 – uber comfortable, stylish t – shirt. Our team of over 120 volunteers were trained to promote sales within their networks.We reached out to fashion influencers and thrift stores for brand visibility.GreenTee has also garnered a substantial instagram presence of 800+ followers upon its launch.We have impacted an audience of 900+ people through seminars and upskilling workshops on sustainable practices, designed to draw attention to our project.

Achievements
25+ Women directly Empowered
10,000+ Plastic bottles recycled
1100+ T-shirts Manufactured
Helped beneficiaries raise Rs.185,000 from sales.
Achieved 12 Sustainable development goals
TECH SHIKSHA
Project Tech Shiksha, launched in December of 2020, was inclined towards slightly different goals. The project served to impart holistic learning to the under-privileged and poverty-ridden youth, whose education had come to a still-stop due to Coronavirus concerns. A significantly huge band of volunteers, with a count spanning in increasing double digits, eagerly volunteered to carry the initiative forward, through customised curriculums conducted over several online classes. In this vein, two distinct sessions on the terrors of plastic waste, as well as the necessity of sustainability, were conducted with the kids involved in the project.
Achievements

Achievements
120+ kids and women directly impacted and educated via upskilling workshops and educational classes
Conducted one-on-one classes in technical subjects such as math and science, as well as stimulating workshops in arts and crafts for more than 50 underprivileged children and students who lost access to education as a result of the pandemic.
ONLINE AWARENESS AND UPSKILLING WORKSHOPS
Enactus Jai Hind College 2022-2023 Annual Report
Faculty Advisor – Dr. Ruchi Chaturvedi
Jai Hind College strictly prohibits sexual harassment in accordance with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition, and Redressal) Act, 2013. Sexual harassment is a punishable offense. The college has established an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) to address any issues related to sexual harassment.
Faculty Advisor : Dr.Ruchi Chaturvedi

Zodiak Fest: An annual two-day event where Rotaract Clubs across Mumbai gather for competitions. Day one features ground events, while day two showcases dance and fashion shows, each year themed differently as a legacy project.Silent Saturdays: A month-long initiative held in November, focusing on a selected slum. Each week targets a specific cause—Health, Education, Hunger, and Safety—with activities like street plays every Saturday to raise awareness.RCJC Model United Nations: Held in December, this two-day debate invites college students to discuss international issues across four committees.Cheekh: An International Women’s Day initiative addressing women trafficking and promoting empowerment through awareness and advocacy.World University Fair: Organized by RCJC in collaboration with Jai Hind College’s Career Counseling Cell, featuring 49 universities from around the world for
in collaboration with Jai Hind College’s Career Counseling Cell, featuring 49 universities from around the world for career opportunities abroad.
Faculty Advisor – Mrs Sarita Jai Shankar

The Economics Association of Jai Hind College, with over 110 members, provides a platform for students to apply theoretical knowledge practically. Open to all students intrigued by Economics, it hones skills in research, public speaking, and management.Events:Arthanomics: Arthanomics, the annual Economics festival of Jai Hind College was initiated in 2014 by The Economics Association. It’s a festival that gives young, budding economists a chance to experience the multifarious simulations and events relating to economics in the real world. Since its inception, it has always been a platform for college students to display their passion for economics.Annual Economics Symposium: Intra-college event with paper presentations, a movie-making contest, and parliamentary debates.The Contrarian: A student-run journal for perspectives and discourse, collaborating with Arthanomics for events like
Contrarian: A student-run journal for perspectives and discourse, collaborating with Arthanomics for events like The Contrarian Column.

Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi Sharma
The Jai Hind College Case Study Cohort, established by the Department of Bachelor of Management Studies, aims to find practical and real modern-day solutions to business problems through intensive analysis and creative problem-solving. Supported by faculty and industry mentors, the Cohort promotes case-based learning and problem-solving.

Milestones AchievedJai Hind College represented India at the HSBC/HKU Asia Pacific Business Case Competition 2017 in Hong Kong. The team, including Upamanyu Acharya, Diveek Jain, Pratik Malhotra, and Ishaan Singh, along with Faculty Advisor Dr. Rakhi Sharma, reached the quarter finals, becoming a Top 16 team globally. Representing India, which had one of the most competitive national competitions of any of the teams in Asia Pacific, Jai Hind College is proud of their achievement and hopes to build on our legacy to help next years’ teams progress even further.
Business TrainingThe Case Study Cohort focuses on training students to adopt a business mindset and solve real-world cases analytically. Sessions include insights from mentors, faculty, and industry specialists, promoting hands-on business discussions and solution generation.

The Case Study Cohort-Media and NewsletterThe Cohort creates fresh, informative content across platforms like Instagram, LinkedIn, and Facebook. Their weekly newsletter, CSC Weekly, covers business themes, corporate news, insights, and recommendations in a visually engaging format.
Faculty Advisor : Dr. Reema Julka
All political science students are members of this association.The Political Science Forum organizes events and talks to inform students about important national and international issues. In the 2014-15 academic year, the forum hosted a guest lecture on ‘Human Rights’ by Dr. Rashmi Oza and a talk on the “Socio-Political & Economic Dimensions of Globalization” by Mr. Arun Ferera. The popular ‘Youth Parliament’ event provided students with insights into parliamentary proceedings. Ten of our students were selected by Mumbai University for the state-level competition.

Faculty Advisor- Dr. Ruchi Chaturvedi
Psychology is one of the most sought after courses at Jai Hind College. The Psychology Association offers budding psychologists a platform to gain new perspectives and develop their skills. It fosters unity within the department and engages students with events like Chitrakatha, Manthan, and Mindscape. Through passionate leadership and diverse activities, the association attracts many students from across the other courses of the college.
Faculty Advisor: Ms. Safina Rakhangi

Throughout the years, Jai Hind College’s Nature Club has been actively engaging students in various environmental initiatives. Beginning with a memorable Nature Trail to Uttan in December 2013, accompanied by Mr. Udhav, Ms. Leena, and Dr. Devangi, the club sparked enthusiasm among commerce students for nature observation. In February 2014, a screening of the BBC documentary “Broken Tail” raised awareness about tiger conservation, emphasizing their crucial role in ecosystems. Further activities included a talk on environmental pollution and plastics in August 2014, a tree plantation drive in collaboration with CSR and NSS, and a sale of medicinal plants in September. The club’s upcoming excursion to Vishnubaug in Badlapur promises further exploration of Maharashtra’s rich flora and fauna, continuing its commitment to environmental education and conservation efforts.

Faculty Advisor- Ms. Safina Rakhangi
The History Association of Jai Hind College is a dynamic group comprising faculty and elected students, fostering both academic and real-life skills. Notable activities from 2018-2019 include a research methodology workshop by Dr. Preeta Nilesh, field visits to the CSMVS museum with interactive sessions on Indian heritage, community outreach projects with various NGOs, and a gender sensitization workshop featuring the film “Boys who like Girls” followed by discussions and documentary production on consent. In 2019-2020, the association organized an Annual Student Seminar on “History from the Lens of Indian Cinema” and participated in a RUSA-sponsored conference at Xavier’s College, with students presenting papers selected for peer-reviewed publication.
Faculty Advisor: Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani

Faculty Advisor: Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani
The Jai Hind College Accountancy Association highlights the significance of accounting, current industry trends, and the opportunities for accountancy students. Focused on enhancing learning beyond the classroom, the Association hosts a variety of events throughout the year. Each year, four distinguished events are meticulously organized—two per semester—spanning seminars, interactive sessions, and more. At the heart of these initiatives lies Elevation, the Association’s flagship event. This grand one-day affair is a celebration of international accounting day, featuring a mix of activities, engaging games, quizzes, seminars to offer students an unparalleled and enriching educational experience.

The Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiative at Jai Hind College fosters a spirit of togetherness and unity among students, aiming to give back to society and spread awareness about social issues. This initiative brings together students eager to work for social causes, making them socially active and responsible citizens. Notable events conducted by the CSR department include the voter registration campaign that spread awareness about the importance of voting and distributed registration forms. Another significant event was the tree plantation drive, conducted in collaboration with the Gymkhana and other committees, where students planted trees on ‘A Road’ and ‘B Road’.On March 2, 2023, the CSR department hosted “Conference Reinvented,” a series of challenging yet insightful rounds focusing on social situations. Participants debated, deciphered, and delivered their inferences,

yet insightful rounds focusing on social situations. Participants debated, deciphered, and delivered their inferences, identifying their communication strengths and weaknesses while proposing solutions to social issues and stigma. The CSR committee also organized a visit to the David Sassoon School and Orphanage. Their goal is to foster holistic student development through community service. The N.S.S Unit engages in numerous social events and projects in collaboration with organizations and NGOs. These initiatives include addressing civic issues with BMC, assisting civilians at police stations, promoting women empowerment and gender equality, conducting cleanliness and blood donation drives, and raising awareness about cancer, AIDS, hepatitis, road safety, and anti-tobacco campaigns.They host the socio-cultural program “UMEED” in partnership with an NGO to spread awareness and happiness. Their involvement extends to participating actively in rallies, seminars, workshops, and inter-collegiate competitions.Additionally, they enthusiastically celebrate Independence Day and Republic Day and organize a seven-day residential camp.

The Philosophy Association started in the academic year 2014-15. The aims and objectives of the association are as follows:● Promoting awareness of current ethical dilemmas.● Fostering the spiritual growth of each student.● Cultivating a critical and logical perspective on life.● Advancing knowledge and combating ignorance.● Helping each student discover their full potential.● Advocating for a balanced and wholesome lifestyle

The Women’s Development Cell is a dedicated initiative focused on addressing the holistic needs and aspirations of female students. By organizing a variety of activities throughout the year, the cell aims to empower women, promote their well-being, and create a supportive environment.The cell prioritizes the physical and mental health of female students by providing information and resources on critical issues such as cervical cancer, first aid, nutrition, and anemia.Through events like Nari Mukti Divas, the cell cultivates a platform for women’s voices to be heard, promoting self-esteem, confidence, and leadership skills.The cell’s annual Nari Mukti Divas celebration serves as a cornerstone of its efforts, bringing together the student community to celebrate women’s achievements and address pressing issues through creative expressions like performances and plays.

Faculty Advisor : Ms Mahek Punjabi

ConCourse is the dynamic guest lecture series and speakers’ committee of the BAF, BBI, and BFM departments at Jai Hind College. It brings industry leaders like Craig Travasso, Tarang Gupta, Guru Gauranga Das, Sanjay Singh, Malay Damania, Nikunj Biyani, and many more to share insights on diverse topics like entrepreneurship, finance, ethical hacking, corporate crisis management, motorsport fundamentals, and mental health. Designed to go beyond textbooks, ConCourse offers interactive sessions that provide real-world knowledge, networking opportunities, and career guidance. By blending education with industry exposure, it equips students with the skills and perspectives needed to navigate their professional journeys with confidence.

Launched in June 2021, this club immerses students in filmmaking through screenings, discussions, and projects. It helps them learn film analysis, production, and storytelling, deepening their media skills.

Constellation is a distinguished media conclave organized by the students of Jai Hind College’s Mass Media Department. It offers a deep dive into the media industry through interactive sessions with experienced professionals.Over the past editions, Constellation has included industry insiders like actors Adil Hussain, Amole Gupte, Saahil Shroff alongside artist Manish Kharage and writers like Mayank Tewari and Rahul Desai. It aims at covering diverse fields such as Improv and Public speaking, AI Specialisation, Public Relations, Journalism, Costume Design, Meme Marketing, and more. Constellation is committed to creating the most compelling and informative mass media conclave in the college for the media’s excellence and curious student minds.

Jai Hind College’s BMM department’s annual Mass Media festival is ‘Detour.’ An extravaganza with a legacy of over one and a half decade, ‘Detour’ promises a blend of friendly competition, fun and an abundance of memories (in the form of DSLR images and video montages). It encompasses a variety of events such as dance, drama, band, fashion show, quizz, J.A.M., etc. as well as enjoyable pre-events like the arrival of the ‘minions’ of ‘Despicable Me 4’ in the Jai Hind Campus (2024) and social activities to give back to the community.
Faculty Advisor: Mr. Wilson Rao

Faculty Advisor: Mr. Wilson Rao
The Dot Com Club organized an outstanding tech fest this academic year, Cyberstrike 2025, with the theme “Genesis and Exodus.” The event was a perfect combination of technical, gaming, and cultural activities that attracted participants and audiences alike. The fest featured thrilling gaming competitions, including popular titles like Valorant, FIFA, BGMI, and VR games, offering an exciting platform for gamers to showcase their skills. On the cultural side, there were engaging events such as a ramp walk, canvas painting sessions, and an intriguing escape room that added a creative and interactive element to the fest.

In addition to the fest, the club also demonstrated its commitment to social responsibility through E-Waste 2024, a CSR initiative that aimed to raise awareness about electronic waste management. As part of this initiative, the club successfully collected an impressive 454 kg of e-waste from institutes across South Bombay. The activity was further enriched by informative seminars and conferences, providing valuable insights into the importance of sustainable practices and environmental conservation. The combination of innovative events and meaningful initiatives made this year’s activities by the Dot Com Club truly remarkable.
Faculty Advisor – Mrs. Yasmin Singaporewala

Faculty Advisor – Mrs. Yasmin Singaporewala
Entourage is the premier intercollegiate financial festival of Jai Hind College’s Department of BAF, BBI, and BFM. True to its name, Entourage brings together the brightest financial and creative minds for two days of intense competition, strategic thinking, and corporate exposure. With engaging pre-events and a decade-long legacy, the fest challenges participants to navigate real-world financial complexities while showcasing their innovation, problem-solving skills, and cultural talents. More than just a competition, Entourage is an exciting platform for learning, networking, and pushing boundaries in a dynamic and electrifying environment.

The Family Managed Business Hub provides a centralized platform for collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and networking among family-owned enterprises. It serves as a supportive ecosystem where these businesses can address common challenges, exchange insights, and foster synergies. Such a hub facilitates the continuity and growth of family businesses by offering resources, mentorship, and a sense of community. Additionally, it contributes to the preservation of family values and legacies, creating a robust network for mutual support and shared success.

The Department of Biotechnology held its annual festival, Genaces 2023—Bioflix, on January 25, 2023, promoting scientific temperament and entrepreneurship among students. Coordinated by S.Y. B.Sc. students with support from F.Y. and T.Y. students, it featured activities like The Big Shot Game Show and Bioheist, drawing over 140 participants. Special guest Ms. Krishma Shah, cofounder of Clinibiz and a Forbes-featured young entrepreneur, added to the event’s success.

The Green Club of Jai Hind College is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting environmental awareness and sustainability. Through initiatives like “Plantonate,” a plant donation drive, the club actively engages students and the community in creating a greener future. Their focus spans from encouraging the cultivation of medicinal and ornamental plants to fostering a deeper connection with nature. The Green Club not only educates about environmental issues but also empowers individuals to take concrete actions towards sustainability. By organizing various activities and campaigns, they strive to make a positive impact on campus and beyond, inspiring a new generation of eco-conscious citizens. Their commitment to a safer, cleaner, and more sustainable environment reflects the club’s vision of a healthier planet for all.

It may also organize career fairs, workshops, and networking events to connect students with industry professionals. Kalki: A Cultural Gathering is the intercollegiate arts festival of the English Association, founded and manifested for the first time on September 11th and 12th, 2015.Kalki emerged from a collective, deep desire to create a college festival that truly allowed every person to explore his or her creativity in a non-judgmental space. The Social and Dramatic Union (SDU) is the premiere cultural body of Jai Hind College, and serves as a powerhouse of creativity, spirit, and student leadership. As the leading cultural committee, SDU not only nurtures artistic talent within the college but also proudly represents Jai Hind at inter-collegiate cultural festivals across Mumbai, bringing home accolades and making a mark wherever we go. SDU also gives a chance to students across all streams to learn working as a team  and exhibit their management skills in organising various events.

Throughout the year, SDU curates some of the most awaited events on campus. These include the fun-filled annual Fresher’s Day, Rose Day, Teacher’s Day Celebration, Children’s Day, the exciting theme launch, Achievers’ Nite , and finally, SHOUTT (Show Us The Talent), our flagship inter-collegiate festival that brings together hundreds of students to compete, collaborate, and showcase their talents. With vibrant performances, powerful dramatics, literary excellence, and artistic flair, SDU creates platforms where students don’t just perform, they shine.

Jai Hind College’s Training & Placement Cell serves as the cornerstone for student career development. The cell’s mission is to equip students with essential technical knowledge, soft skills, and confidence necessary for career success. To achieve this, they offer tailored skill development workshops covering crucial areas such as CV building, communication, body language, and interview strategies. Students benefit from hands-on internship experiences with leading companies, gaining practical insights into their chosen fields. The cell’s robust recruitment drives, featuring over 100 prestigious companies, provide students with diverse placement opportunities. To foster the appreciation of Indian art, culture, and heritage among students, Jai Hind College aims to set up a new forum, an Art, Music and Culture Club/Centre (JAMCC) at our premises which will enhance co-curricular activities on the campus and build interest among the students to immerse in art, dance and music. Through these activities, students will gain insight into the diverse art forms prevalent in India and contribute to their preservation and promotion. They will develop cross cross-cultural approach by engaging in events which showcase inhouse students’ talents and allow them to curate creative events that teach diversity and need to preserve the same. Additionally, they will have the opportunity to engage in cultural volunteer work with organizations dedicated to promoting cultural harmony and learning, engaging in saving rare art forms or showcasing them in commitment to

to promoting cultural harmony and learning, engaging in saving rare art forms or showcasing them in commitment to sustainability. Courses on Music, dance, musical instruments, Rabindra Sangeet and studio and folk art will be offered under the club auspices. Two annual events, one dance based and one music based (Kalanidhi I and Kalanidhi II (temporary Suggestion)) will be held when students can showcase their talent.

Promotion of Indian Classical Music, dance and Culture
About SPICMACAY (Society for the Promotion of Indian Classical Music and Culture Amongst Youth) is a non-political, nationwide, voluntary movement founded in 1977 by Dr. Kiran Seth, Professor-Emeritus at IIT, Delhi who was awarded the ‘Padma Shri’ for his contribution to the arts in 2009. SPICMACAY’s intention is to enrich the quality of formal education by increasing awareness about different aspects of Indian heritage and inspiring the young mind to imbibe the values embedded in it. It is an apolitical, non-profit, voluntary movement, committed to increasing awareness of Indian culture & classical art forms, Committed to Preserving & promoting India’s intangible heritage with focus on Youth and Students, rooted in Schools & Colleges to enhance quality of education through arts.

It seeks to inspire youth through experiencing the mysticism embodied in the rich and heterogeneous cultural tapestry of Indian and World Heritage, with a hope that the beauty, grace, values, and wisdom embodied in these arts will influence their way of life and thinking and inspire one to become a better human being.
About the SPIC MACAY Heritage Club

The primary aim of the club is to raise awareness about the diverse facets of Indian culture, including its regional variations, folk traditions, and indigenous practices. It encourages students to immerse and know about the rich heritage and encourage them to adopt interest in art and creativity which is important for holistic growth of the students. The club seeks to delve into the lesser-known aspects of Indian arts and folk styles, shedding light on their historical significance and contemporary relevance and encourage students to volunteer to take up activities to save art and artists. Through curated events and workshops, the club aims to showcase the beauty and complexity of Hindustani music and contemporary Indian art forms, fostering a deeper appreciation among the students. To enrich the cultural landscape of the college, the club organizes live performances, lecture

among the students. To enrich the cultural landscape of the college, the club organizes live performances, lecture demonstrations, and interactive sessions with renowned artists and scholars. Recognizing the importance of both tangible artifacts and intangible cultural practices, the club endeavors to safeguard India’s rich heritage for future generations.

The Ministry of Tourism celebrated this year at India@75 by launching the campaign focusing on “YUVA Tourism Club” with the strategic objective of creating interest, awareness and a sense of responsibility towards our country’s natural and cultural heritage / tourism amongst students and youth. The vision is to nurture and develop young ambassadors of Indian tourism who would become aware of tourism possibilities in India, appreciate our rich cultural heritage and develop an interest and passion for tourism. These young ambassadors at Jai  Hind College will be managed by an Executive team from students of B.Voc Travel and Tourism Management along with  interested students from all streams who  would be catalysts in  promoting sustainable  tourism practices, experiences and heritage  in India and in the region.

The Chemical Society of Jai Hind College offers a vibrant platform for students to explore, learn, and engage with the fascinating world of chemistry. Through initiatives like ChemTalk, students connect with experts and gain insights into contemporary developments in the field. With a balanced focus on knowledge sharing, skill development, and social responsibility, the Chemical Society strives to inspire a new generation of chemists equipped to make meaningful contributions to science and society.

Code of conduct
OBJECTIVE
To encourage mutual respect among students, teachers, and staff regardless of background, fostering a safe and inclusive atmosphere, creating a focused learning environment thus maintaining the dignity and public image of the institution through appropriate conduct of its members.
Students shall wear their identity cards at all times while present on college premises.
The University of Mumbai mandates a minimum of 75% attendance in each semester to be eligible for examinations; however, the college expects students to maintain attendance levels as high as possible beyond this requirement.

The use of mobile phones within college premises— classrooms, libraries, laboratories,  corridors—is strictly prohibited unless permitted under a teacher’s supervision.
During examinations, mobile phones must be switched off and kept aside to prevent any unfair practices.
Maintaining discipline across the campus is expected of every student.
Any damage to college property will result in a monetary fine.
Ragging in any form is strictly prohibited on campus and is considered an anti-social and criminal act, liable to rustication and legal proceedings.
Students are not permitted to bring outsiders onto the campus without prior written permission from the college authorities.
The following activities are prohibited and will invite strict disciplinary action
Any activity carried out without proper authorization that goes against the ethos of the college or harms its reputation.

Disrespect, unruly behavior, use of harsh words and defiance shown towards teaching, non-teaching and/or administrative personnel. Threatening to physically harm or physically harming a fellow student, teaching or non-teaching staff
Disrespect, unruly behavior, use of harsh words and defiance shown towards teaching, non-teaching and/or administrative personnel.
Threatening to physically harm or physically harming a fellow student, teaching or non-teaching staff
Discriminatory remarks on grounds of gender, race, religion, and caste.
Possession, display or use of weapons and ammunition.
Possession, consumption, and distribution of alcohol, prohibited substances, smoking/vaping
Any form of physical coercion and/or unwelcomed sexual behavior or taking sexual advantage of another person without consent

The use of mobile phones on campus for recording lectures or conversations, taking photographs without consent, or playing loud music.
The use of mobile phones on campus for recording lectures or conversations, taking photographs without consent, or playing loud mus
ic.
Academic dishonesty in any form.
Teachers’ Guidelines
Teachers of this academic institution are subject to the guidelines founded by UGC for college teachers. As per the UGC directives, every college teacher assumes the responsibility to conduct oneself in accordance with the standards of this profession.
Practices Towards Students
Be committed towards the needs of students and sensitivity to satisfy the querent’s mind
Be conscientious and dedicated towards academic and administrative tasks
Avoid pre-conceived notions, prejudices and biases during classroom interaction.

Avoid pre-conceived notions, prejudices and biases during classroom interaction.
Build a healthy and interactive educational environment
Treat all students equally irrespective of academic performance, caste, class, religion, gender, and place of origin. Avoid any partiality or vindictive attitude towards learners
Conform to the ethos of the profession and act in a professional and dignified manner
Practices Towards Institution
Teachers must update their knowledge and skills by attending various courses, workshops, conferences, seminars, etc. so that students, departments, and the institution benefit from their growth.
Classroom punctuality ought to be maintained.
Besides teaching, teachers should participate in research activities and inculcate research attitude in students.

Besides teaching, teachers should participate in research activities and inculcate research attitude in students.
Teachers should adopt updated pedagogy and innovative methodology to keep abreast with changes in the global academic scenario.
Teachers shall complete administrative responsibilities entrusted to them besides carrying out extra-curricular and co-curricular programmes.
There should be no conflict between professional, private, and personal practices such as avoiding taking tuitions in the concerned subject which might impact their quality of classroom teaching.
Information regarding all aspects of examination should strictly be kept confidential.
Avoid addressing personal matters during working hours.
Practices Towards Colleagues
Teachers should be respectful, courteous, and cooperative towards junior and senior colleagues.

Teachers should be respectful, courteous, and cooperative towards junior and senior colleagues.
Collective efforts should be taken to complete academic and administrative goals.
Leadership roles should be assumed or accepted whenever required.
Teachers should refrain from making false allegations on colleagues for personal vendetta.
Non-teaching staff should be treated respectfully.
Strictly maintain professional relationships between colleagues.
Code of Conduct for Non-Teaching and Support Staff
The code of conduct is also applicable to the non-teaching and support staff of the college.
Professional Conduct
Maintain punctuality and should not be under the influence of drugs or alcohol at work.
Familiarise and adhere to the rules and policies of the institution.
Be sincere and earnest towards the duties assigned to them and also help one another when required.

Be sincere and earnest towards the duties assigned to them and also help one another when required.
No other employment or financial commitment to be made outside college premises during work hours.
Maintain apolitical environment and take all internal issues to the Principal to sort out.
Communication between colleagues, students and teaching staff should be polite, avoiding harsh tone and words.
Non-teaching staff should maintain confidentiality of records regarding administrative and examination matters.
When non-teaching staff are entrusted with confidential documents for transportation, photocopying or other responsibilities, they should be cautious not to leak this information.
Perform duties with integrity, veracity and without boundaries of discrimination of caste, class, religion and gender.

Perform duties with integrity, veracity and without boundaries of discrimination of caste, class, religion and gender.
Non-teaching staff should behave in a professional and helpful manner in libraries, science laboratories, computer laboratories and administrative offices towards students and teaching staff.

UNIVERSITY OF FRASER VALLEY:Jai Hind College (Autonomous) has a tie-up with the University of the Fraser Valley, British Columbia, Canada. The University of the Fraser Valley (UFV) is an accredited public Canadian University and is a member of AUCC (Association of Universities and Colleges of Canada). Also, UFV was ranked as the top public university in British Columbia for quality teaching, best student-faculty interaction and best career preparation.

CARLETON UNIVERSITY:Carleton University’s Canada-India Centre and Jai Hind College to offer joint certification programs in International Relations, Cybersecurity, Business Management, Data Analytics, Entrepreneurship and Faculty Exchange opportunity.
RITSUMEIKAN ASIA PACIFIC UNIVERSITY (APU):is Japan’s first genuinely international university. Our College has signed an MoU with the University to provide opportunities to our students for further studies after completion of graduation. The MoU is an endeavour to contribute to development of human resource and in commitment to achievement of sustainable growth in the region.
STANFORD UNIVERSITY VISIT:
On 27th March, 2019 a group of Stanford undergraduate students visited Jai Hind College as part of their trip to Mumbai, on special initiative by our Principal, Dr Ashok Wadia.

SUMITOMO SCHOLARSHIP:Sumitomo Corp. is a Japanese corporation that gives academic scholarships to a selected few college in India, Jai Hind College being one of them. They award a scholarship of Rs.20,000/- each to a total of 30 students of Degree college (aided & unaided sections). The forms of applicants are scrutinised and based on consistent academic performance students are short-listed. The short-listed applicants are then interviewed by Sumitomo Corp. representatives and 30 students are finally selected. These students are felicitated on Achievers’ Nite
ASSOCIATION OF ASIA SCHOLARS (AAS) :
Jai Hind College and AAS are associated by an MoU for our International Relations Course

TATA CONSULTANCY SERVICES (TCS):A purpose-led organization that is building a meaningful future through innovation, technology, and collective knowledge. A part of the Tata group, India’s largest multinational business group, TCS has over 500,000 of the world‟s best-trained consultants in 46 countries. The company generated consolidated revenues of US $22.2 billion in the fiscal year ended March 31, 2021, and is listed on the BSE (formerly Bombay Stock Exchange) and the NSE (National Stock Exchange) in India. TCS’ proactive stance on climate change and award-winning work with communities across the world have earned it a place in leading sustainability indices such as the MSCI Global Sustainability Index and the FTSE4Good Emerging Index. Our Collaboration with TCS is on our Programs such as MSc Big Data and Bachelor of Business Administration.

Both parties shall work on co- creation model so that entrepreneurs (student & alumni body) can be created & supported. The selected students / alumni will get access to infrastructure, expertise, networks and entrepreneurship ecosystem of the Parties on specific approval basis. Organization of workshops, networking events, & conferences; exchange visits for entrepreneurs and mentors involved with specific Program/s. Mentoring, industry expertise, and investors networks to the extent possible. Early feedback/ adoption of the start-up solution as end user/s‟. Both incubation centres can exchange talent and infrastructure resource (only on the basis of approval). Incubatees and pre-incubatees of both centres will have access to workshops, programs, conferences, seminars etc. organized by both centres individually and jointly.

INSTITUTE OF RISK MANAGEMENT :The IRM is the leading professional body for Enterprise Risk Management. IRM helps build excellence in risk management to improve the way organisations work. They provide globally recognised qualifications and training, publish research and thought leadership and set professional standards, which define the knowledge, skills and behaviours today’s risk professionals need to meet the demands of an increasingly complex and challenging business environment. IRM members work in many roles, in all industries and across the public, private and not-for-profit sectors around the world. Jai Hind College has partnered with the Institute of Risk Management – India Affiliate to create opportunities for undergraduate students to pursue IRM’s professional examinations. (Only for level 1) IRM, the world leader in enterprise risk management examinations, provides an ideal

examinations. (Only for level 1) IRM, the world leader in enterprise risk management examinations, provides an ideal pathway (Level 1 to Level 5) for becoming a risk intelligent leader / certified fellow and chief risk officer, recognized across the globe in 143 countries.

Wadhwani Opportunity: Empowers millions of students with core skills/soft skills to improve employability and higher wages. TOURISM AND HOSPITALITY SKILLS COUNCIL : is a Not-for-Profit Organization, registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860, promoted by the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) with inclusive representation of the Government, Industry, Industry Associations and Training Institutes across India. Formed by the Industry and for the industry to tackle the skilling of large manpower to fulfil the industry requirements, THSC plays a crucial role in bridging this ever-growing gap. We have a mandate to create a robust and sustainable eco-system for skill development in the industry, catering to all the sub sectors of the industry, namely, Hotels, Tour Operators, Food Service Restaurants, Facilities Management and Cruise Liners. For our Bachelor of Vocational Education-Travel and Tourism Management, we have MoU with THSC for NSQF alignments, curriculum value addition and certifications.

OBSERVER RESEARCH FOUNDATION (ORF) :is an independent global think tank based in Delhi, India. The foundation has three centres in Mumbai, Chennai and Kolkata. ORF provides potentially viable inputs for policy and decision-makers in the Indian Government and to the political and business communities of India. Propelled by the process of reforms initiated in the 1990s, ORF, over the past 30 years of its existence, has effectively narrated and participated in India‟s story as the country has acquired an unmistakable global footprint. From primarily looking inward and engaging with domestic reforms, to gradually forging global partnerships, ORF today plays a seminal role in building political and policy consensus that enables India to interact with the world. ORF helps discover and inform India‟s choices. It carries Indian voices and ideas to forums shaping global debates. It provides

discover and inform India‟s choices. It carries Indian voices and ideas to forums shaping global debates. It provides non-partisan, independent, well-researched analyses and inputs to diverse decision- makers in governments, business communities, and academia and to civil society around the world. The mandate undertaken by them is to conduct in-depth research, provide inclusive platforms and invest in tomorrow‟s thought leaders today. ORF has been associated with us for last eight years on our International Relations Course.

NATIONAL MARITIME FOUNDATION :The NMF was accordingly established, in 2005, as the nation‟s first maritime think-tank for the conduct of independent and policy- relevant research on all „matters maritime‟. It is a policy-relevant research organisation of acknowledged excellence that would not only undertake serious, cutting-edge maritime research of its own but would also provide a common platform for advocacy, discourse, and debate, between maritime-related national and international institutions, organisations, and academic establishments, as also reputed and renowned individuals, professionals, and practitioners the world over. Jai Hind College and NMF are associated by an MoU for our International Relations Course.

Our partnership with Thomas cook is to enhance and hone the students‟ skills for the Tourism Industry and provide internships and placements opportunities to our students.

LCGC CHROM CONSUMABLES : LLP Jai Hind College has recently signed an MoU with LCGC Chrom Consumables LLP on the 27th of May, 2022. From The Principal’s Desk
Dear Students, Faculty, and Esteemed Stakeholders,
I welcome you, and extend my warmest greetings from the Principal’s desk at Jai Hind College.
We, at Jai Hind College, are committed to providing you with a comprehensive and enriching educational experience. Our dedicated faculty has explored a wide range of subjects, ensuring that you receive a holistic education that prepares you for success in all aspects of life.

Education is the key to unlocking one’s true potential, as the famous Irish poet, William Butler Yeats once said, “Education is not the filling of a pail, but the lighting of a fire”. We firmly imbibe this principle in our teaching methodologies. Our faculty members are passionate about imparting knowledge that empowers students such as you to make a positive impact on society. Through interactive lectures, practical sessions, and collaborative projects, we aim to foster critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills in all our students.

The recently introduced National Education Policy (NEP) has brought about significant changes in the Indian education system by focusing on holistic development, multidisciplinary learning, and skill enhancement. At Jai Hind College, we have embraced NEP’s principles, and are dedicated to nurturing well-rounded individuals who possess not only subject knowledge but also the ability to adapt, innovate, and contribute meaningfully to society.
I am delighted to share that Jai Hind College has been granted “Empowered Autonomous College” status by the University of Mumbai in 2023. This recognition symbolizes our commitment to academic excellence and gives us the freedom to design innovative programs and curricula that align with the needs of the industry and society, and provide our students with a competitive edge.

As we continue on this journey of excellence, we remain grateful for the unwavering support from all our stakeholders. Together, we will strive to nurture and empower the next generation of leaders, innovators, and change-makers.
In conclusion, I would like to quote Nelson Mandela’s views on education: “Education is the most powerful weapon.” Let us harness the power of education to create a better future, not only for ourselves but for the entire community. Together, we can make a difference.
Thank you for your continued trust in Jai Hind College.
Warm regards,
Prof. (Dr.) Vijay Dhabholkar
Principal, Jai Hind College

Legacy
Jai Hind College, Empowered Autonomous, Mumbai, has a history spanning  several years since it’s inception in 1948. Soon after the Indian Independence, a group of visionary professors of D.J. Sind College, Karachi, and other eminent educationists founded Jai Hind College under the registered name of ‘Sind Educationists’ Association’ primarily for the educational rehabilitation of the Sindhi community. Their goal was to contribute their expertise, knowledge, and skills in order to set up an educational institution par excellence.

From a humble Arts college, Jai Hind College soon diversified into Science in 1949, followed by Commerce in 1980. Post 2000, several Self-financed and Vocational Programs were introduced to improve employability of students, such as Bachelor of Management Studies (BMS), Bachelor of Mass Media (BMM), Bachelor of Accounting and Finance (BAF), Bachelor of Banking and Insurance (BBI), Bachelor of Financial Market (BFM) and, Bachelor of Vocational Studies in Travel & Tourism Management (B. Voc. TTM) and Software Development (B. Voc. SD).Programs such as Bachelor of Digital Strategy (BDS) , Master of Science in Big Data Analytics and Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA)  in collaboration with TCS as the Industry partner,Bachelor In International Accounting (BIA) And Bachelor In Actuarial Science And Quantitative Finance (BAQF) were recently introduced. Inclusion of such Industry aligned

In Actuarial Science And Quantitative Finance (BAQF) were recently introduced. Inclusion of such Industry aligned Programs are periodically assessed based on feedback from all stakeholders to help align academics with current market demands.

Our notable alumni, such as Mr. Sunil Dutt,Mr.Piyush Goyal,Ms.Supriya Sule,Dr.Raghunath Mashelkar,Mr.Vikram Nankani,Mr Ajay Piramal, Ms Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Mr. John Abraham ,Mr. Vishal Dadlani, Mr.Shaan and many others began their illustrious journeys at Jai Hind College, proving that students can achieve national and global recognition with our foundation.
The Union Ministry of HRD and the University of Mumbai conferred on us the prestigious Empowered Autonomous Status for a period of ten years from the academic year 2023-24.
Over the years , Jai Hind College has empowered students to achieve their dreams while staying true to the value systems of the Founding Fathers.

About
The history of Jai Hind College library dates back to 1948 with the inception of Jai Hind College. The library was named in 1969 as the “T.M. Advani Memorial Library” in loving memory of the late Principal T.M. Advani.
The library has a rich collection of books consisting of a wide variety and subscribes to daily journals, periodicals, and magazines. The library also has a comprehensive reference collection of encyclopedias, handbooks, dictionaries, atlases, etc.

The Dewey decimal classification system is followed to classify books in the library. The library has provided Open Access Facility since the 90s. It has a wide range of novels in different languages and other light reading materials catering to diverse interests. The library collection includes languages such as English, Marathi, Hindi, Sindhi, Gujarati, Sanskrit, and French.
The library has a Reading Room Committee to support the functioning of the library and facilitate its development plans by advocating library development activities. The committee’s structure is as follows: Chairperson – Librarian; Members – teaching staff members from different streams and students. The purpose of the Reading Room Committee is to act as a channel of communication and dialogue between the library and its users.

The library provides access to NLIST. All e-resources subscribed for the college under the NLIST project are now accessible to the library through the NLIST website. Remote access to these resources includes databases, e-journals, and e-books accessible to faculty, students, and researchers free of cost. Library members have to register to avail the N List personal user ID and password. The registration link for this membership is provided separately. Accessing and downloading articles is possible directly from the publishers’ websites. Jai Hind Library has been featured in the Top 10 users list of NLIST.

The College library was named the Principal T.M. Advani Memorial library

Code of Conduct
Students using the College Library are required to observe the following rules:
1. A Library Membership Card is essential for entry.
2. Strict silence must be observed.
3. Eating and beverages are not permitted, or a fine will be charged.
4. Cell phones must be switched off, or a fine will be charged. In case of study purposes, permission must be obtained from the Reference section staff.

5. Listening to music or using cell phones with earphones is not allowed.
6. Cleanliness and neatness must be maintained.
7. No scribbling on tables or chairs. A fine will be levied.
8. Seats cannot be reserved by depositing belongings.
9. A vacated seat can be retained for a maximum period of ten minutes only.
10. No responsibility will be taken for any item left behind.
11. Students have to deposit their bags, personal books, and notebooks at the property counter against a token. Cell phones and money purses should not be kept in the bag by the students. The library staff is not responsible for any loss.
NON-OBSERVANCE OF THE ABOVE WILL RESULT IN DEBARMENT FROM THE LIBRARY.

CIF (Center for Research Facilities)
Facilities
Newsletter
CIF requisition Form
Virtual tour
The Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) at Jai Hind College, inaugurated on 14th July 2015, is a state-of-the-art laboratory housing sophisticated analytical instruments designed to support advanced scientific research. These instruments have been procured through generous funding from DST-FIST, UGC, and the college management. The facility is accessible to faculty and students of the college and is also extended to external researchers. It serves as a hub for student and faculty research, while also hosting hands-on training workshops and internship programs, fostering skill development and innovation.
The list of instruments housed in the Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) is as follows:

The list of instruments housed in the Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) is as follows:
1. Differential Scanning Calorimeter2. High-Performance Liquid Chromatography with VWD3. FTIR Spectrometer4. Spectrofluorimeter5. Uv-vis Spectrometer6. Rotary Evaporator7. Binocular Microscope with LED8. Refrigerated Centrifuge9. Gel Documentation10. Gel Rocker11. Fraction Collector12. Melting point apparatus13. Double distillation unit14. Karl Fischer Apparatus15. Overhead stirrer16. Magnetic stirrer with temperature sensor17. Weighing Balance (1mg and 0.1 mg)18. Bath Sonicator

About Us
At Jai Hind College, our Training & Placement Cell is the nexus of skill development and career
opportunities, providing unparalleled support to students. With the backing of our esteemed
faculty, we connect students with top-tier internships and placements.
Our Mission
We aim to cultivate industry-ready professionals equipped with technical knowledge, soft skills,
and confidence to excel in their careers.
Opportunities We Provide
– Skill Development: Tailored workshops on CV building, communication, body language, and
interview strategies.
– Internships: Hands-on experience with 80+ leading companies.

– EdTech

– Banking

At Jai Hind College, we are committed to fostering holistic development for our students through a comprehensive mentoring initiative. This initiative aims to guide and support our students in both their academic and professional journeys by pairing students with experienced faculty members and industry experts who provide personalized guidance. This mentorship is designed to help students navigate their educational paths, develop critical skills, and prepare for successful careers.
Mentorship Modes

Mentorship Modes
Face-to-Face Mentoring: Conducted in a classroom setting, these sessions allow students to interact directly with their mentors. This personal interaction helps in building strong relationships and provides a conducive environment for detailed discussions and feedback.
Conducted in a classroom setting, these sessions allow students to interact directly with their mentors. This personal interaction helps in building strong relationships and provides a conducive environment for detailed discussions and feedback.
Virtual Mentoring: Utilizing modern technology, virtual mentoring sessions are arranged to provide flexibility and accessibility. This mode ensures that mentoring is not limited by geographical constraints and allows for continuous support.

Utilizing modern technology, virtual mentoring sessions are arranged to provide flexibility and accessibility. This mode ensures that mentoring is not limited by geographical constraints and allows for continuous support.
Mentoring Benefits
Personalized Guidance: Each student receives tailored advice and feedback, helping them to identify and achieve their individual goals.
Each student receives tailored advice and feedback, helping them to identify and achieve their individual goals.
Professional Development: Industry experts share insights and real-world experiences, preparing students for the demands of the professional world.
Industry experts share insights and real-world experiences, preparing students for the demands of the professional world.
Academic Support: Faculty mentors assist with academic planning, subject-specific challenges, and research opportunities.

Faculty mentors assist with academic planning, subject-specific challenges, and research opportunities.
Networking Opportunities: Students can build a network of contacts in their field of interest, opening doors to internships, job opportunities, and collaborative projects.
Students can build a network of contacts in their field of interest, opening doors to internships, job opportunities, and collaborative projects.
Our Mentors
Our mentors are a blend of esteemed faculty members and distinguished industry professionals who bring a wealth of knowledge and experience, ensuring that students receive a well-rounded perspective.
How it works
Students interested in getting mentoring can contact the program coordinator, where mentorship pairings are made based on the student’s field of study, interests, and career aspirations.

At Jai Hind College, we believe that effective mentoring is critical to student success. We are committed to empowering students, helping them reach their greatest potential, and preparing them for the challenges and possibilities of the future. We have a demonstrated track record of achievement, with many students reaching significant milestones in their academic and professional careers.

This committee serves as a platform for students to voice their grievances regarding any aspect of campus life, such as infrastructure issues, dissatisfaction with faculty, administrative inefficiencies, or other concerns. The committee ensures that student complaints are heard promptly, investigated thoroughly, and addressed through appropriate measures. It acts as a bridge between students and the administration, ensuring transparency, accountability, and a responsive campus environment that works to maintain student satisfaction and well-being.

Swayam
SWAYAM is a programme initiated by Government of India and is designed to achieve the three cardinal principles of Education Policy viz., access, equity and quality. The objective of this effort is to take the best teaching learning resources to all, including the most disadvantaged. NPTEL is one of the 9 national coordinators which offer MOOCs in diverse areas like Architecture & Planning, Education, Engineering & Technology, Humanities & Arts, Law, Management & Commerce, Math & Sciences. Students can take up MOOCs offered by NPTEL-SWAYAM to claim credits in their academic program at Jai Hind College Autonomous.

Secoond  Merit  List  -  FYBBI  (2026  -  2027)
 
Students  whose  names  appear  in  the  First  Merit  List  for  the  Bachelors  of  Commerce  Banking  &  Insurance  program  are  provisionally  selected  for  admission,  subject  to  fulfillment  of  all  eligibility  criteria  and  successful  completion  of  the  document  verification  process.  All  candidates  whose  name  appear  on  the  Merit  List  must  login  to  the  Jai  Hind  College  Admission  Portal  and  complete  the  following  steps:
 
1)  Upload  the  required  documents  as  specified  in  Annexure  'B'

1)  Upload  the  required  documents  as  specified  in  Annexure  'B'
 
2)  Upload  the  University  of  Mumbai  Pre-Admission  Enrollment  Form  (This  is  mandatory  for  admission  processing)
 
3)  Upload  the  signed  Undertaking  Form  as  per  Annexure  'C'  ( This  confirms  provisional  admission  as  per  University  of  Mumbai  guidelines)
 
Students  will  receive  an  official  email  invitation  with  details  of  a  virtual  interaction  and  document  verification  session  with  the  Admission  Committee.  Following  successful  verification,  the  Admission  Portal  will  be  activated  to  enable  online  fee  payment.

Important  Note  :  Name  on  the  list  does  not  guarantee  admission,  subject  to  producing  necessary  documents.  Candidates  are  strongly  advised  to  adhere  to  all  deadlines  and  instructions  provided.  Failure  to  comply  will  result  in  cancellation  of  the  Provisional  Admission  offer. Third  Merit  List  -  FYBFM  (2026  -  2027)
 
Students  whose  names  appear  in  the  First  Merit  List  for  the  Bachelors  of  Commerce  Financial  Markets  program  are  provisionally  selected  for  admission,  subject  to  fulfillment  of  all  eligibility  criteria  and  successful  completion  of  the  document  verification  process.  All  candidates  whose  name  appear  on  the  Merit  List  must  login  to  the  Jai  Hind  College  Admission  Portal  and  complete  the  following  steps:
 
1)  Upload  the  required  documents  as  specified  in  Annexure  'B'

1)  Upload  the  required  documents  as  specified  in  Annexure  'B'
 
2)  Upload  the  University  of  Mumbai  Pre-Admission  Enrollment  Form  (This  is  mandatory  for  admission  processing)
 
3)  Upload  the  signed  Undertaking  Form  as  per  Annexure  'C'  ( This  confirms  provisional  admission  as  per  University  of  Mumbai  guidelines)
 
Students  will  receive  an  official  email  invitation  with  details  of  a  virtual  interaction  and  document  verification  session  with  the  Admission  Committee.  Following  successful  verification,  the  Admission  Portal  will  be  activated  to  enable  online  fee  payment.

Important  Note  :  Name  on  the  list  does  not  guarantee  admission,  subject  to  producing  necessary  documents.  Candidates  are  strongly  advised  to  adhere  to  all  deadlines  and  instructions  provided.  Failure  to  comply  will  result  in  cancellation  of  the  Provisional  Admission  offer.   For  any  queries  contact:  admissions.commerce.mgmt@jaihindcollege.edu.in
 
For  complete  details  and  required  documentation  please  refer  to  the  official  Admission  Notice  dated  05.5.2026

Job Description – IT Administrator 
   
1 
 
Job Title  :  College IT Administrator / IT 
Services  
Job Type     : Full – Time  
Job Location : Jai Hind College, Mumbai 
Department  : IT Operations 
Scope of Work :  
The College IT Administrator is responsible for the day -to-day administration, 
maintenance, and support of the college’s information technology systems, computer 
laboratories, end-user devices, network services, and related digital resources. This 
role ensures smooth and secure routine IT operations across academic and 
administrative functions while working under the guidance of the IT Head and in 
coordination with faculty, office staff, students, and external vendors. 
 
Key Responsibilities:  
I ] IT Infrastructure Support & Support to Stakeholders: 
• Provide first-level and second -level IT support for faculty, staff, students, and

• Provide first-level and second -level IT support for faculty, staff, students, and 
administrative offices in relation to desktops, laptops, printers, projectors, 
software, and campus IT services. 
• Administer and maintain computer labs, classroom systems, user accounts, 
shared systems, and routine software installations, updates, and 
troubleshooting. 
• Assist in monitoring the college network, internet connectivity, Wi -Fi access, 
antivirus status, system health, and basic security compliance. 
• Coordinate routine backups, basic recovery procedures, hardware servicing, 
preventive maintenance, and vendor follow -up for annual maintenance or 
support requirements. 
• Escalate infrastructure -level issues, policy matters, procurement needs, and 
major technical risks to the IT Head 
• Understand client needs, provide updates, and offer training

major technical risks to the IT Head 
• Understand client needs, provide updates, and offer training 
• Be responsible for maintaining, expanding, and scaling our site 
• Maintain good rapport with internal & external Stakeholders, maintaining  a calm 
demeanour to resolve any Customer queries 
 
 
 
 
           Job Description – IT Administrator 
   
2 
 
 
 
II ] Data Management, Documentation & Reporting:  
• Maintain records of IT assets, service requests, software licenses, 
configurations, and support activities for institutional reference and audit 
readiness 
• Maintain comprehensive documentation for database architecture, processes, 
and configurations 
• Produce regular reports on database performance, availability, and usage 
metrics 
• Present technical updates and recommendations to senior management 
 
 
III ] Digital Platform Implementation & Support:

III ] Digital Platform Implementation & Support: 
• Support the implementation and smooth functioning of institutional digital 
platforms such as ERP, LMS, email, online examination support systems, and 
related academic technologies, as assigned. 
 
 
Preferred Qualifications :  
• Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field 
with any Microsoft Network / Administration Certifications 
 
 
Required Skills and Competencies: 
 
• Working knowledge of desktop support, operating systems, office 
productivity software, hardware troubleshooting, and user support 
processes. 
• Familiarity with campus networking, Wi -Fi management, device configuration, 
data backup practices, and endpoint security controls. 
• Ability to maintain documentation, coordinate with vendors, and support 
academic and administrative users in a service-oriented manner.

academic and administrative users in a service-oriented manner. 
• Good communication skills, reliability, discretion with institutional data, and the 
ability to work within defined reporting structures. 
• Certification in any of the above Skill sets listed will be preferred

JAI HIND COLLEGE 
BASANTSING INSTITUTE OF SCIENCE & J. T. LALVANI COLLEGE OF COMMERCE and

11/07/2026 
SY Degree Admissions for the Academic Year 
2026-2027 
(SY – BMS/BBA) 
Online Admissions for the Academic Year 2026 —2027 for all SY BMS & 
SYBBA Degree programs for In-house students to take admissions to the next 
higher class will commence as per the dates mentioned below, subject to 
fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. 
1. Pending Cases of Eligibility at the University: 
 Students who were admitted to the First year of Degree Programs from 
Other Boards (Other than Maharashtra State HSC Board) during 2025 - 2026 
(for BMS & BBA) and whose cases are pending for University Eligibility 
due to Non- submission of the 12 th Mark Sheet, verification report, passing

due to Non- submission of the 12 th Mark Sheet, verification report, passing 
certificate, transfer certificate & migration certificate, the names of those 
students w ill not appear in the roll call  till they submit the ir pending 
documents for eligibility. 
2. Details required under Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) 
The University Grants Commission , New Delhi and University of Mumbai 
(Cir. No. DBoEE / ICF / 2022 -23 / 14) have mandated the implementation 
of Academic Bank of C redits (ABC). As per the National Education Policy 
2020, ABC is a national -level facility to promote flexibility of curriculum 
framework and provide academic mobility to students with an appropriate 
credit transfer mechanism.

Therefore, it is mandatory fo r all the students to generate an 
ABC ID which needs to be submitted to the University of 
Mumbai. In order to create ABC IDs students are advised to 
follow the steps that are uploaded on our college website. 
3. Online Admission Process: 
● The Admission proces s will be Online and the Admission link along 
with the Username and Password will be shared with the students via 
email from the Jai Hind College ID and an SMS from "DVSLAD". This 
link will be activated for students from 11th July, 2026. 
● The students must follow the instructions carefully and submit a 
duly completed admission form according to the timeline/schedule 
announced here under. 
●  For the security of your admission & payment of fees, it is advised not 
to share your personalized Log in ID and Password with anyone.  
4. Schedule for the Admission Process: 
 Class Date And Time

5. Step by Step guide to complete your admissions for SY Programs:  
 
1) The students will receive an email from the Jai Hind College ID and an SMS 
from "DVSLAD." Click on URL - 
(https://datavista.in/cms/student/Int/auth/login.php?sectionmaster_Id=26 ) 
2) This communication will include the above-mentioned URL and login 
credentials for accessing the Student Portal. 
3) Students should keep all relevant documents ready in Digital format before 
starting the Admission process.

4) Log in to the Admission Portal and carefully follow the instructions to 
complete all sections of the form, including student information and 
document uploads. 
5) Once all sections are filled, proceed to submit the online Application Form 
by making a successful payment of Rs 500/- form fees. 
6) After successful payment of Admission Form fees, an Application Number 
will be generated. 
7) Once the Application Number is generated, the option to pay the course fees 
will be enabled. Click 'Pay Fees' and proceed towards payment of college 
fees. Upon successful payment, you will be able to print the 
acknowledgment/invoice. Students can make online payment only by Net 
Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card and UPI. There will be no bank charges 
for making online payments of College Fees. 
8) Complete the Admission process by paying the course fees to secure your 
admission.

8) Complete the Admission process by paying the course fees to secure your 
admission. 
9) After completion, download the Admission Form (PDF) and payment 
receipts for future reference. 
10) If the admission form PDF does n ot open on the portal, disable the pop up 
blocker in your browser to download the documents.

Note . 
a) In the event of failure of transaction, transaction aborted, double 
payment or non -generation of online fees receipt students are 
requested to register c omplaint on 
admission.fees@jaihindcollege.edu.in 
b) For any queries related to Scholarship students please contact 
Ms. Swati / Ms. Poonam personally in the Office, Main Building, 
Ground Floor. 
c) In case of any technical difficulties while filling online form  or 
uploading documents, students can reach out to Online Helpdesk 
in the Portal by clicking on the Blue button at the bottom right 
of the Screen.
